metadata.description: Web application dedicated to Istex sub-corpus creation and extraction.

Header.baseline:
Create and download your scientific corpus

First step of your text and data mining project

SearchSection.resultCount: {count, plural, =0 {<resultsSpan>#</resultsSpan> documents found} =1 {<resultsSpan>#</resultsSpan> document found} other {<resultsSpan>#</resultsSpan> documents found}}

AssistedSearchInput.valueTooltip: Enter only one value. Metacharacters such as * or ? are not allowed.

Dialog.title: Are you sure?
Dialog.content: By confirming your modifications, you will not be able to continue with the query building assistant.

ImportInput.placeholder:
Paste your list of identifiers* (ARK, DOI, Istex ID) or import a .corpus file
* only one format at a time

PromptModal.title: Search in natural search
PromptModal.introduction: Describe your problem in natural language. Your description will be automatically converted into an Istex Search compatible Lucene query.
PromptModal.note: The currently supported fields are the title, the abstract, the author keywords, the publication date, the language and the document type (research article). The queries are automatically generated from your description and can vary from one generation to another.
PromptModal.placeholder: Research articles in English about nuclear energy published after 2015

gateways.title: Tune your download to your use case
gateways.subtitle: Istex Search offers connectors for corpus analysis tools.
gateways.paragraph: No matter your use case (data curation, semantisation, statistics, phylogenetic reconstructions, etc.), Istex Search allows to download your corpus in formats compatible with text and data mining tools.
gateways.buttonTop: For more information about these tools:
gateways.gatewaySuggestion: Want to suggest a connector for a tool?

DownloadSection.title: Download your data in 3 steps
DownloadSection.body: Metadata of publications in the Istex repository are available to everyone. The full texts and the enrichments created by Istex are available to members of French higher education and research institutions.

downloadSteps.title: Create a query
downloadSteps.body: Multiple search modes are available to query the Istex repository: the simple search, the assisted search and the import of an identifier list.

downloadSteps.title: Browse the results
downloadSteps.body: Various filters and indicators are available to analyse the corpus' content and tune your query to get a high-quality corpus.

downloadSteps.title: Massively download
downloadSteps.body: Istex Search allows you to download up to {maxSize, number} documents and choose the data types (metadata, full texts, enrichments) in formats tailored to your needs.

CourseSection.title: Need to better understand Istex Search?
CourseSection.body: The Istex team offers trainings on the corpus creation methodology.

results.unavailableTitle: This feature is not available in this search mode.

formatLinks.openAccess: View this document on its source platform
formatLinks.istexView: View the XML TEI document and its enrichments in Istex View

MemoModal.title: Query with Lucene
MemoModal.subtitle: Lucene is the query language used by the Istex search engine (Elasticsearch). In the simple search mode, the Lucene syntax allows you to efficiently target documents you are interested in.

general.title: General
list:
By default, querying is case insensitive but diacritic sensitive.
évolution ≠ evolution

Querying multi-term expressions requires double quotes.
"speech therapy"

A whitespace is equivalent to an OR operator.
fungi OR fungus = fungi fungus

operators.title: Operators
list:
AND (or &&) returns the documents that contain all the queried terms.
cinéma AND Chaplin

OR (or ||) returns the documents that contain at least one of the queried terms.
Greenland OR subarctic

+ can be used directly in front of a term to make it required.
+cinéma +Chaplin

NOT returns the documents that do not contain the queried term.
linguistics NOT syntax

- (or !) can be used directly in front of a term to exclude it.
linguistics -syntax

fields.title: Fields
list: The list of fields is available in the <fieldsLink>tutorial appendix</fieldsLink>.

Querying a specific field is done with the technical name followed by a : (e.g. title, publicationDate, doi).
title:Rimbaud
If no field name is provided, all fields are queried.

The exact querying of a field is possible thanks to .raw (e.g. host.title.raw).
title.raw:"Verlaine, Flaubert et Rimbaud"

metacharacters.title: Metacharacters * and ?
list:
The * metacharacter replaces 0 up to n character(s).
title:mémoris*

The ? metacharacter replaces 1 character.
title:m?mori?ation
The * and ? metacharacters are not allowed in quoted queries.

parentheses.title: Parentheses
list:
Parentheses allow you to prioritise the operations and create groups.
(Luke OR Leia) AND Skywalker
title:(plastic NOT bertrand)

ranges.title: Querying by ranges
list:
You can query by ranges with square brackets.
publicationDate:[2015 TO *]
title.raw:'{ape TO appliance}'

boosting.title: Boosting
list:
The ^ followed by a number metacharacter allows you to give more weight to a term.
"star wars"^2 skywalker
Boosting affects the results order but not their count.

fuzzy.title: Fuzzy search
list:
The ~ metacharacter allows you to query variants of a term (2‑character difference max).
title:mémorisation~2

proximity.title: Proximity search
list:
The ~ followed by a number metacharacter allows you to query an expression that contains 2 terms with a distance in between.
title:"reading child"~10
title:"return jedi"~2

regex.title: Regular expressions
list:
Regular expression must be written in between // to be interpreted.
title:/[frc]at/

consentModal.title: Cookies
consentModal.description: We use cookies and collect data to improve the experience on our website.

sections.title: Cookies
sections.description: We use cookies and collect data to improve the experience on our website.

sections.title: Stricly necessary cookies
sections.description: These cookies are required for the website to function properly.

body.name: NEXT_LOCALE
body.description: Cookie to store the display language.

body.name: cc_cookie
body.description: Cookie to store your cookie preferences.

sections.title: Analytics
sections.description: These cookies collect information about how you use our website via Matomo. All data is anonymised and cannot be used to identify you.

body.name: _pk_id.*
body.description: These cookies are used by Matomo to distinguish users by giving them an identifier.

body.name: _pk_ses.*
body.description: These cookies are used by Matomo to distinguish users by giving them a unique session.

sections.title: More information
sections.description: To get more information, you can visit our privacy policy.

CopyButton.error: Could not write to your clipboard.
CopyButton.badEnv: This feature is not available in this environment.

NotFoundPage.title: Oops, page not found!
NotFoundPage.body: It tooks like you've reached a dead end… The page you are looking for doesn't exist (anymore). Here are some options to find your way:
list: Go back to the Istex Search home page to make a new query.
Use the assisted search to get assistance in querying the Istex repository.
NotFoundPage.contact: If you believe this is a mistake, please <link>contact us</link> to let us know.

errors.GetAggregationError: A problem occurred while fetching the values.

custom.label: Custom usage
custom.description: Choose the data types to download (full texts, metadata, enrichments, appendices or covers) and their format.

lodex.label: Lodex
lodex.description: Open‑source web application designed for structured data, which allows you to visualise and enrich your data and turn it into a website.

cortext.label: Cortext
cortext.description: Open‑source software designed for the mining and analysis of heterogeneous text corpora, offering a range of automated processing functions (clustering, visualisation of temporal data, etc.).

gargantext.label: GarganText
gargantext.description: Open‑source software designed for the collaborative exploration of document collections, combining tools for natural language processing, complex network analysis and interactive data visualisation.

nooj.label: NooJ
nooj.description: An application designed for corpus analysis, enabling the creation of linguistic resources (dictionaries, grammars) and their use for annotation or querying purposes (semantic analysis, concordances, information extraction, etc.).
